diagrams.title: Contiguous Memory Allocation Types
diagrams.description: Shows fixed partitioning vs variable partitioning memory layouts
elements: Fixed Partitioning: Equal-sized partitions with internal fragmentation shown
Variable Partitioning: Dynamic holes and allocated blocks of varying sizes
Process A: 200KB, Hole1: 50KB, Process B: 150KB, Hole2: 100KB
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how memory is divided and allocated in different contiguous allocation strategies

diagrams.title: Address Translation in Contiguous Allocation
diagrams.description: Shows how logical address is converted to physical address using base register
diagrams.purpose: Understand memory protection and address translation mechanism

diagrams.title: Fragmentation Types
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of internal and external fragmentation
elements: Internal Fragmentation: Partition box larger than Process box with shaded wasted area
External Fragmentation: Scattered holes (10KB, 20KB, 15KB) unable to fit large process (40KB)
Compaction result: One large contiguous hole after rearrangement
diagrams.purpose: Differentiate between types of memory fragmentation and understand compaction effect
